assertion_results.evidence: Line 8: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — new entry present with serena_ui as Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: Line 9: 'serena_backend — Already configured in Repository Registry' with 'Status: EXISTING'. Line 13: 'serena_ui — NEW, not present in Repository Registry' with 'Status: NEW — added to Registry'.

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 20-33 preserve the naming convention text, mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol example, and serena_backend limitation. Only addition is line 34: 'serena_ui: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 25-26: 'User was asked whether to enable security triage configuration' / 'User declined — no Security Configuration section added'. claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section.
